courses.name: Introduction to European and Russian Studies
courses.description: An introduction to the study of Europe and Russia, including aspects of the histories, societies, cultures, and politics of the region.

courses.name: European, Russian and Eurasian Politics, Society and International Affairs
courses.description: An interdisciplinary examination of the domestic issues facing the countries of Europe, Russia and Eurasia and the position of these countries in a global context, including geopolitical, economic, security and human dimensions.
courses.prerequisites: second year standing.

courses.name: Europe, Russia and Eurasia Beyond Borders: Literature and Culture
courses.description: An exploration of the cultural borders and boundaries of contemporary Europe, Russia and Eurasia. Using literary and visual texts, the course explores issues such as migration, cultural and political borders and their transcendence, cultural responses to authoritarianism, the Cold War and its afterlives, and memory.
courses.prerequisites: second year standing.

courses.name: Co-operative Work Term
courses.prerequisites: registration in the B.A. European and Russian Studies (Honours) Co-operative option, completion of the Co-op preparation classes offered by the Co-op Office and permission of the Institute.

courses.name: Post-Soviet States and Societies
courses.description: The relationship between social forces and state structures at both the national and local levels in the USSR and the post-Soviet states.
courses.prerequisites: fourth-year Honours standing or permission of the Institute.

courses.name: Nationalism in Russia and Eurasia
courses.description: Ethnic basis of nationalism in the region. Ethnic politics and trends.
courses.prerequisites: fourth-year Honours standing or permission of the Institute.

courses.name: Nation-Building in Central and Eastern Europe
courses.description: Processes of nation building in the region examined in terms of a particular country, or set of countries.
courses.prerequisites: fourth-year Honours standing or permission of the Institute.

courses.name: The Balkans in Transition – 1918 to 1989
courses.description: The seminar uses the concept of transition to understand the Balkan encounter with modernity and Europe. Key periods to be examined include the interwar era and the period of communist rule, with an emphasis on political, social and economic themes.
courses.prerequisites: fourth-year Honours standing or permission of the Institute.

courses.name: The Balkans since 1989
courses.description: Selected topics in Balkan politics and society since the collapse of communism in 1989, focusing on the democratic transition and the EU accession process.  The legacies of communist rule, democratization and the many national questions that still exist in the region.
courses.prerequisites: fourth-year Honours standing or permission of the Institute.

courses.name: The Great Russian Novel
courses.description: A study of masterpieces of prose fiction from the Golden Age of Russian literature.  Readings will be chosen from writers such as Turgenev, Tolstoy, Dostoevsky, Gogol, and/or others.  All texts will be studied in English translation.
courses.prerequisites: Third-year standing.

courses.name: European Integration and European Security
courses.description: Issues related to the formation of supra-national decision-making structures in Europe.
courses.prerequisites: fourth-year Honours standing or permission of the Institute.

courses.name: Selected Topics in European Integration Studies
courses.description: Selected topics related to European integration in the post-World War II period.
courses.prerequisites: fourth-year Honours standing or permission of the Institute.

courses.name: Russia’s Regional and Global Ambitions
courses.description: Domestic conditions in Russia from 2000 to the present and the framing of Russia’s foreign policy and strategic objectives towards the former Soviet republics and other key global actors, including the United States, the European Union, NATO and China.
courses.prerequisites: fourth-year Honours standing or permission of the Institute.

courses.name: Special Topics in European Studies
courses.description: A seminar focusing on selected topics related to Europe.
courses.prerequisites: fourth-year Honours standing or permission of the Institute.

courses.name: Special Topics in Russian and Eurasian Studies
courses.description: A seminar focusing on selected topics related to Russia and neighbouring countries.
courses.prerequisites: fourth-year Honours standing or permission of the Institute.

courses.name: Central Europe, Past and Present
courses.description: Evolution and current status of Central Europe from periods of foreign control in the late nineteenth and twentieth centuries to independent statehood, with  emphasis on national accommodations and conflicts.
courses.prerequisites: fourth-year Honours standing or permission of the Institute.

courses.name: Politics of Identity in Europe and the Russian Area
courses.description: The relationships between political transformation, identity-building, ethnicity, and gender politics in post-communist states, considered in comparison with select countries in Central and/or Western Europe.
courses.prerequisites: Fourth-year Honours standing or permission of the Department and one of PSCI 2101 , PSCI 2102 , PSCI  2500, PSCI 3208 , PSCI 3209 , PSCI 3500, PSCI 3502 .

courses.name: Internship and Applied Policy Skills
courses.description: A seminar accompanying an unpaid internship placement to develop workplace and applied policy skills. Relating applied experience to academic literature. Writing skills for an applied policy setting. Internship placement: 12 days over l2 weeks.
courses.prerequisites: open only to fourth-year EURUS B.A. Honours students with a minimum B+ average and placement in an internship position in the same semester or in the previous semester (based on a competitive application process).

courses.name: Politics of Central Eurasia
courses.description: Examination of the Caucasus and Central Asia, from Chechnya to former Soviet republics of the region, Afghanistan and Chinese Turkestan. Interests of Russia, China, and the United States. Emphasis on underdevelopment, oil and gas, terrorism, Islam.
courses.prerequisites: fourth year Honours standing or permission of the Institute.

courses.name: Foreign Policies of Soviet Successor States
courses.description: The foreign policies of the USSR and of Russia and selected other successor states, with special emphasis on the search for a new security order.
courses.prerequisites: fourth-year Honours standing or permission of the Institute.

courses.name: Politics of the Caucasus and Caspian Basin
courses.description: Examination of the South Caucasus (Azerbaijan, Georgia, Armenia), the Russian-held North Caucasus, including Chechnya, and relations with Iran. Emphasis on state and society, oil and gas, transregional communications, interests of western powers, ethnic relations.
courses.prerequisites: fourth-year Honours standing or permission of the Institute.

courses.name: EU Summer Study Abroad
courses.description: This course is open only to students in approved summer study options in Europe, particularly the EU Study Tour.
courses.prerequisites: approval of the Institute.

courses.name: Contemporary Europe: From Postwar to the European Union
courses.description: History of contemporary Europe from 1945 to present covering both eastern and western halves of the continent and including social, cultural, political, and economic dimensions.
courses.prerequisites: fourth-year Honours standing or permission of the Institute.

courses.name: Europe and International Migration
courses.description: Europe’s role in international migration. Topics to be discussed may include migration and mobility as both assets and challenges for sending, transit, and destination countries, changing geographies of migration, inclusion and exclusion, political mobilization, and responses of European states and other actors.
courses.prerequisites: fourth-year Honours standing or permission of the Institute.

courses.name: Imperial Russia and the Russian Revolution
courses.description: Examination of the expansion and downfall of tsarist Russia from the eighteenth century to the revolutionary era and the establishment of Bolshevik rule.  Topics include the relationship between the monarchy and subject peoples, social and economic change, and daily life.
courses.prerequisites: fourth-year Honours standing or permission of the Institute.

courses.name: The Soviet Union: Power and Culture
courses.description: Examination of the rise of the Soviet Union to a global power and subsequent tensions that promoted its collapse.  The course will analyze Stalinism, the Second World War, the Thaw, and Brezhnev and Gorbachev eras through the lens of the USSR’s citizens.
courses.prerequisites: fourth-year Honours standing or permission of the Institute.

courses.name: The Business Environment in Europe
courses.description: The economic, political, legal, and cultural environment for doing business in the European Union and other regions in Europe. Patterns of foreign trade and investment, market characteristics, science and technology, regulation and European integration, and business culture.
courses.prerequisites: third-year standing.

courses.name: Tutorial in European and Russian Studies
courses.description: Tutorials or reading courses on selected topics may be arranged with the permission of the Institute and agreement of the instructor.
courses.prerequisites: permission of the Institute.

courses.name: Honours Essay
courses.description: Individual research project resulting in a major essay, completed under the supervision of a faculty member and evaluated by the supervisor and a second reader. Students should consult with the Supervisor of Undergraduate Studies regarding the topic and supervisor. Institute’s Honours Essay guidelines apply.
courses.prerequisites: fourth-year standing, a CGPA of 9.00 or higher in courses qualifying for credit in European and Russian Studies, and permission of the Institute.

free_electives.degree: European and Russian Studies B.A. Honours (20.0 credits)
European and Russian Studies B.A. Combined Honours (20.0 credits)
free_electives.text: 11.  Sufficient free electives to make 20.0 credits for the degree.

free_electives.degree: European and Russian Studies B.A. (15.0 credits)
Specialization in Europe and Russia in the World B.G.In.S. Honours (20.0 credits)
Stream in Europe and Russia in the World B.G.In.S. (15.0 credits)
